observed_failure.surface: installed SkillGuard self-host after its installation receipt was captured
observed_failure.prior_local_claim: the installed SkillGuard could self-host after installation currentness was established
observed_failure.observed_result: self-host first called the canonical-source runtime boundary directly; after that call site was fixed, closure's execution-depth receipt replay independently called the same canonical-only boundary and rejected the active installation's exact .sg-runtime/installation receipt subtree

classification.owner: SkillGuard self-host and execution-depth closure runtime fingerprint selection

root_cause_backpropagation.supported_cause: the installed-supervisor repair introduced the location-derived execution boundary, but self-host and its downstream execution-depth closure replay remained sibling consumers of the canonical-only fingerprint
root_cause_backpropagation.would_have_failed_if: the affected-sibling review had enumerated every direct and downstream runtime fingerprint consumer and replayed installed self-host through final closure after receipt capture
root_cause_backpropagation.new_model_item: every executing runtime consumer, including supervisor and self-host, must derive the boundary from its exact execution location; canonical identity comparison remains explicit only in installation verification
root_cause_backpropagation.owner_code_contract: guard_execution_runtime_fingerprint -> self-host fingerprints, claimed run, shared execution-depth runtime identity, depth receipt, and closure replay

generalized_repair.rule: Self-host and every downstream runtime receipt consumer use one non-selectable, location-derived execution boundary; an explicitly bound current runtime identity may be replayed but callers cannot select a looser boundary.
generalized_repair.observed_regression: installed SkillGuard self-host passes with only its exact installation verification subtree present
generalized_repair.same_class_regression: source or staged self-host with .sg-runtime remains blocked, and active installation with unexpected runtime siblings remains blocked
generalized_repair.negative_boundary: the repair does not ignore arbitrary runtime output, remove installation receipts, add a caller-selectable mode, or weaken canonical installation parity

required_revalidation: portable boundary observed and same-class regressions
SkillGuard affected tests and FlowGuard checks
transactional reinstall and installation receipt replay

revalidation_evidence.focused_tests: 30 passed plus 14 subtests for execution depth, self-host, closure, and portable runtime boundaries
revalidation_evidence.flowguard: validation composition 19/19 positive gates and 7/7 known-bad gates; current-runtime authority model passed
revalidation_evidence.full_test_mesh: aggregation sha256:283cf0014bf133864f11aa1b2ed0fe9ef4bfc83b0ab7622d3db3d62dc80485b7; execution_count 0; installation and global-prompt bindings current

claim_boundary: The repair is validated for current SkillGuard source-to-install parity and installed self-host closure. It does not prove external target skills, publication, release, or future AI behavior.
